card.intuition: A standardized naming system (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) that communicates exactly what kind of changes are in a new release of a library or service.
card.analogy: A menu update: **Patch** is fixing a typo in the price. **Minor** is adding a new soup to the list (New feature, old orders still work). **Major** is changing from a Sushi bar to a Taco stand (Breaking change, old orders no longer work).
when_to_use: Communicating API changes to external clients
Managing internal shared library updates between teams
gotchas: 0.y.z: versions before 1.0.0 are considered 'Experimental'. You can break anything at any time without changing the Major version.
Don't release breaking changes in a Minor update! You'll break thousands of projects that use the `^` or `~` symbols in their dependencies.

exercise.prompt: If you add a new feature that DOES NOT break existing code, which part of the version (X.Y.Z) do you increment?
